title: Standard conjecture of Künneth type with torsion coefficients
speaker: Junecue Suh
abstract: A. Venkatesh asked us the question, in the context of torsion automorphic forms: Does the Standard Conjecture (of Grothendieck's) of Künneth type hold with mod p coefficients? We first review the geometric and number-theoretic contexts in which this question becomes interesting, and provide answers: No in general (even for Shimura varieties) but yes in special cases.

proof_stages.label: Classical rational-coefficient standard conjectures
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls cycle class maps, the Hodge conjecture, the action of cohomology classes on cohomology by correspondences, and Grothendieck’s standard conjecture that the rational Künneth projectors are algebraic.

proof_stages.label: Mod-p Künneth-projector question
proof_stages.summary: Replacing Q by F_p still allows correspondence actions and mod-p Künneth projectors via Poincaré duality, leading to Venkatesh’s question whether these projectors can be algebraic, with torsion caveats noted.

proof_stages.label: Shimura-variety target and correspondence supply
proof_stages.summary: The mod-p question is sharpened for compact Shimura varieties and related to the availability of Hecke correspondences, analogously to Frobenius correspondences in the Katz–Messing finite-field case.

proof_stages.label: Torsion incidence and non-algebraicity theorem
proof_stages.summary: The invariant I_p(X) is defined as the first degree where reduction to F_p fails to be onto, equivalently where p-torsion appears in homology, and finiteness of I_p(X) is stated to force non-algebraicity of pi_I, pi_{2n-I}, and their sum.

proof_stages.label: Abundance of counterexamples and Shimura consequences
proof_stages.summary: The speaker states that varieties with prescribed I_p and dimension exist, often even rational and simply connected, and then applies the obstruction to compact Shimura surfaces with I_p=1.

proof_stages.label: Torsion-free pivot and Jacobian affirmative theorem
proof_stages.summary: After isolating the topological torsion obstruction, the talk asks about torsion-free cohomology and states a positive result: for Jacobians of curves the mod-p Künneth projectors are algebraic for every p, with a low-dimensional principally polarized abelian variety corollary.

proof_stages.label: Symplectic-invariant expression on principally polarized abelian varieties
proof_stages.summary: For a principally polarized abelian variety, polarization and addition define classes e_1, m, e_2 of Künneth types (2,0), (1,1), and (0,2), and symplectic invariant theory expresses each projector as an explicit rational linear combination of their products.

proof_stages.label: Jacobian integrality via Abel–Jacobi and Poincaré formula
proof_stages.summary: When the abelian variety is a Jacobian, the Abel–Jacobi maps from symmetric powers of the curve and Poincaré’s formula identify the required divided powers of the polarization with integral algebraic cycle classes, completing the mod-p algebraicity argument.
